HyFI
  • API Reference
  • hyfi package
  • View page source

hyfi package

HyFI: Hypocenter-based 3D Imaging of Active Faults

A Python package for 3D imaging of active faults based on relatively relocated hypocenter datasets. Based on the methodology described in:

Truttmann, S., Diehl, T., Herwegh, M. (2023). Hypocenter-based 3D Imaging of Active Faults: Method and Applications in the Southwestern Swiss Alps. Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth. https://doi.org/10.1029/2023JB026352

class hyfi.AutoClassConfig(autoclass_bool: bool = True, n_clusters: int = 2, algorithm: str = 'vmf_soft', rotation: bool = True)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration for automatic classification module.

algorithm: str = 'vmf_soft'
autoclass_bool: bool = True
n_clusters: int = 2
rotation: bool = True
validate()[source]

Validate configuration parameters.

exception hyfi.ConfigValidationError[source]

Bases: ValueError

Custom exception for configuration validation errors.

class hyfi.FaultImagingWorkflow(config: ProjectConfig)[source]

Bases: object

Main workflow class for hypocenter-based 3D fault imaging.

This class manages the complete workflow including: - Fault network reconstruction - Model validation with focal mechanisms - Automatic classification - Stress analysis - Visualization and data saving

__init__(config: ProjectConfig)[source]

Initialize the workflow with configuration.

Parameters:

config (ProjectConfig) – Configuration object containing all parameters

get_results_summary() → dict[source]

Get a summary of the analysis results.

Returns:

Summary statistics and key results

Return type:

dict

run_fault_network_only() → Tuple[DataFrame, DataFrame, DataFrame, DataFrame, DataFrame, DataFrame][source]

Run only the fault network reconstruction module.

Returns:

(data_input, data_input_outliers, data_output, df_per_X, df_per_Y, df_per_Z)

Return type:

tuple

run_full_analysis() → dict[source]

Run the complete fault imaging workflow.

Returns:

Dictionary containing all results from the analysis

Return type:

dict

class hyfi.FaultNetworkConfig(n_mc: int = 1000, r_nn: float | str | None = 100.0, dt_nn: float | str | None = 26298.0, mag_type: str = 'ML', auto_optimize_parameters: bool = False, optimization_method: str = 'optuna', optimization_grid_points: int = 25, optimization_n_trials: int = 50, optimization_sampler: str = 'tpe', optimization_n_startup_trials: int = 10, optimization_early_stopping_rounds: int | None = None, optimization_early_stopping_threshold: float = 0.0001, optimization_pareto_sampler: str = 'nsga2', optimization_pareto_population: int = 50)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration for fault network reconstruction module.

optimization_early_stopping_threshold: float = 0.0001
optimization_grid_points: int = 25
optimization_method: str = 'optuna'
optimization_n_startup_trials: int = 10
optimization_n_trials: int = 50
optimization_pareto_population: int = 50
optimization_pareto_sampler: str = 'nsga2'
optimization_sampler: str = 'tpe'
r_nn: float | str | None = 100.0

Validate configuration parameters.

class hyfi.ModelValidationConfig(validation_bool: bool = True, foc_file: str | Path | None = None, foc_sep: str = ';', foc_mag_check: bool = True, foc_loc_check: bool = True)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration for model validation module.

Validate configuration parameters.

class hyfi.ProjectConfig(project_title: str = 'Fault Imaging Analysis', hypo_file: str | Path = '', hypo_sep: str = '\t', out_dir: str | Path = <factory>, fault_network: FaultNetworkConfig = <factory>, model_validation: ModelValidationConfig = <factory>, auto_class: AutoClassConfig = <factory>, stress_analysis: StressAnalysisConfig = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Main configuration class containing all module configurations.

Convert string paths to Path objects and validate basic properties.

Create ProjectConfig from dictionary (for backward compatibility).

Convert ProjectConfig to dictionary (for backward compatibility).

validate()[source]

Validate all configuration parameters.

class hyfi.StressAnalysisConfig(stress_bool: bool = True, S1_trend: float = 301, S1_plunge: float = 23, S3_trend: float = 43, S3_plunge: float = 26, stress_R: float = 0.35, PP: float = 0, fric_coeff: float = 0.75, use_shapefile_stress: bool = False, stress_field_shapefile: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration for fault stress analysis module.

PP: float = 0
S1_plunge: float = 23
S1_trend: float = 301
S3_plunge: float = 26
S3_trend: float = 43
fric_coeff: float = 0.75
stress_R: float = 0.35
stress_bool: bool = True
stress_field_shapefile: str | None = None
use_shapefile_stress: bool = False
validate()[source]

Validate configuration parameters.

hyfi.auto_classification(input_params, df_hyfi, starting_fault_counter=1, sequence_label=None, segmentation_level=None)[source]

Automatic classification of point cloud based on fault orientations.

Parameters:

  • input_params (dict) – Input parameters containing clustering configuration.

  • df_hyfi (DataFrame) – Input dataframe containing all hypocenter data and computed fault parameters. Clustering results will be added as new columns to this dataframe.

  • starting_fault_counter (int, optional) – Starting value for global fault counter (default: 1)

  • sequence_label (str, optional) – Label of the sequence being processed (e.g., ‘A1’, ‘B2’)

  • segmentation_level (str, optional) – Segmentation level letter (e.g., ‘A’, ‘B’, ‘C’)

Returns:

  • df_hyfi (DataFrame) – Input dataframe with added clustering columns and global fault IDs.

  • fault_metadata (list) – List of dictionaries containing metadata for each fault

  • next_fault_counter (int) – Next available fault counter value

hyfi.auto_load_config(file_path: str | Path) → HyFIWorkflowDAG | MultiSequenceConfig | ProjectConfig[source]

Automatically detect JSON file format and load configuration.

For JSON files, detects between DAG format, multi-sequence format, and legacy format.

Parameters:

file_path (Union[str, Path]) – Path to the JSON configuration file

Returns:

Loaded configuration object based on detected format

Return type:

Union[HyFIWorkflowDAG, MultiSequenceConfig, ProjectConfig]

Raises:

ConfigValidationError – If file format is not supported or file cannot be loaded

hyfi.create_template_config(file_path: str | Path, format: str = 'json') → None[source]

Create a template configuration file.

Parameters:

  • file_path (Union[str, Path]) – Path where to save the template

  • format (str) – File format - only ‘json’ is supported

Raises:

ConfigValidationError – If format is not supported or file cannot be written

hyfi.fault_stress(df_hyfi, input_params)[source]

Calculate the stress parameters for each individual earthquake.

Parameters:

  • df_hyfi (DataFrame) – Single dataframe containing fault plane data and results.

  • input_params (dict) – Input parameters for stress analysis.

Returns:

  • df_hyfi (DataFrame) – Input dataframe with added stress analysis columns.

  • S2_trend (float) – Trend of intermediate principal stress.

  • S2_plunge (float) – Plunge of intermediate principal stress.

hyfi.faultnetwork3D(input_params)[source]

Calculate 3D fault network from hypocenters.

Parameters:

  • hypo_file (str) – Path of hypoDD input file.

  • hypo_sep (str) – Separator for hypoDD input file.

  • out_dir (str) – Path for output folder.

  • n_mc (int) – Number of MC simulations.

  • r_nn (int) – Search radius for nearest neighbor search [m].

  • dt_nn (int) – Search time for nearest neighbor search [h].

  • validation_bool (bool) – If True: perform model validation calculations.

  • foc_file (str) – Path for focal mechanism catalog.

  • foc_sep (str) – Separator for focal mechanism catalog.

  • stress_bool (bool) – If True: perform fault stress analysis.

  • S1_mag (int) – Maximum principal stress magnitude [MPa].

  • S2_mag (int) – Intermediate principal stress magnitude [MPa].

  • S3_mag (int) – Minimum principal stress magnitude [MPa].

  • PP (int) – Pore fluid pressure [MPa].

  • S1_trend (int) – Trend of maximum principal stress direction.

  • S1_plunge (int) – Plunge of maximum principal stress direction.

  • S3_trend (int) – Trend of minimum principal stress direction.

  • S3_plunge (int) – Plunge of minimum principal stress direction.

  • stress_R (float) – Stress shape ratio R.

  • fric_coeff (float) – Friction coefficient.

  • autoclass_bool (bool) – If True: perform autoclassification.

  • mag_type (str) – Type of magnitude (ML, Mw)

Returns:

  • DataFrames with the parameters of the input parameters (with/without detected outliers), the full 3D fault

  • network model and the MC hypocenter locations.

hyfi.faults_stereoplot(input_params, data_output)[source]
hyfi.focal_validation(df_hyfi, input_params)[source]

Validate the fault network model with focal mechanism data using single dataframe approach.

Parameters:

  • df_hyfi (DataFrame) – Single dataframe containing hypocenter data and computed fault plane parameters

  • input_params (dict) – Input parameters including validation settings and focal mechanism file info

Returns:

df_hyfi – Input dataframe with added focal mechanism validation columns

Return type:

DataFrame

hyfi.model_3d(input_params, data_input, data_output)[source]

Generate an interactive 3D model with plotly using single dataframe approach.

Parameters:

  • input_params (dict) – Input parameters.

  • data_input (DataFrame) – Input data (legacy parameter, not used with single dataframe approach).

  • data_output (DataFrame) – Output data (legacy parameter, not used with single dataframe approach).

Return type:

Interactive 3D model, saved in the output directory.

hyfi.save_config_to_json(config: ProjectConfig, file_path: str | Path) → None[source]

Save configuration to a JSON file (legacy format).

Note: For DAG configurations, use save_dag_to_json() instead.

Parameters:

  • config (ProjectConfig) – Configuration object to save

  • file_path (Union[str, Path]) – Path where to save the JSON file

Raises:

ConfigValidationError – If file cannot be written

hyfi.save_data(input_params, data_input, data_output, per_X, per_Y, per_Z, config_source_file=None)[source]

Save data in txt-files.

Parameters:

  • input_params (DataFrame) – Input parameters.

  • data_input (DataFrame) – Input data (includes outliers marked with clust_labels = -1).

  • data_output (DataFrame) – Output data.

  • per_X (DataFrame) – X coordinates of MC datasets.

  • per_Y (DataFrame) – Y coordinates of MC datasets.

  • per_Z (DataFrame) – Z coordinates of MC datasets.

  • config_source_file (str, optional) – Path to the original configuration file to copy to output.

Return type:

None.

Submodules

hyfi.cli module

Command Line Interface for HyFI: Hypocenter-based 3D Imaging of Active Faults

This module provides a command-line interface for running fault imaging analyses.
